ops.title: Cookie clobber root cause + v0.14.1 monotone-write fix (2026-07-14/15 incident)
ops.body:
## Incident
Reproducible on every Arc quit/reopen (2026-07-14 evening): a fresh claude.ai login survived while Arc ran, then reverted to a stale July-1 session after relaunch. Sibling cookies advanced; only write-once identity cookies (`sessionKey`/`sessionKeyLC`) froze at `last_update` 12:22:18Z.

## Root cause (verified on deployed cda40dc + synckit 028db96)
1. **Blind write**: `cookie.Write` upserted `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET col=excluded.col` with no comparison against the on-disk row; `applyTo` rewrote the entire union whenever `rowSetEqual` differed on any row.
2. **Huge TOCTOU**: converge extracts all sources first, runs SSH legs (observed 10s–4m46s), then applies — any cookie the browser flushed mid-pass was regressed by that same pass.
3. **Self-perpetuating freeze**: once the disk row was stale, the browser's memory-fresh copy was no longer dirty and never re-flushed; sibling churn kept `rowSetEqual` false, so every pass re-planted the stale row. Write-once login cookies froze; churny cookies self-healed — which is why precisely logins died.
4. v0.13.0's busy gate only amplified (delayed prompt capture); merge itself was honest newest-wins. Secondary vector: `is_persistent = hasExpires` meant synced session/expired rows were planted then purged by Chromium at startup, and a peer logout stamped a fresh-but-dead row that won the merge.

## Fix (v0.14.1, commit a9d21f37d226)
- `cookie.Write` is the correctness boundary: upsert carries `WHERE excluded.last_update_utc > cookies.last_update_utc` (schema-aware; v18 stores without the column stay unguarded). Strict `>`: equal keeps the on-disk row. No content-hash tiebreak in SQL (`encrypted_value` is nonce-randomized). `Write` returns summed `RowsAffected`, so a stale apply reports `applied: 0`.
- Sync path carries only syncable rows (`cookie.Syncable`: persistent AND expiry >= now): filtered in converge's gather (before quarantine/merge/equality snapshot — symmetry by construction) and in the daemon's inbound `handleApply` (mixed-version backstop). Export paths (get_cookies/extract/bridge seeding) deliberately unfiltered.
- Converge warns when a source yields rows stamped >2min in the future (skew visibility; see backlog task "doctor: peer clock-skew probe").

## Review dispositions (opus finder×2 + refuter; codex lane was quota-blocked)
- **E1 accepted**: equal-timestamp different-content rows never converge (perpetual no-op apply nomination). Needs two writes in the same Chrome microsecond on two hosts; the pre-fix "resolution" was the clobber itself.
- **E2 accepted + documented**: the mass-drop quarantine's write-back restore is best-effort under monotone writes — missing keys re-insert, but a key the razed browser regenerated with a fresher timestamp keeps its fresh value (LWW). Force-restore would reopen the clobber vector and need an RPC protocol change. Quarantine's merge exclusion is unchanged. Test-fidelity note: `fakeSource.Apply` in converge_test ignores the guard; the real-store tests in stores_test pin it.

## Operational traps burned into this incident
- **Detached-checkout trap**: the tree sat detached at 1f5e50a (v0.12.0) while 0.13.0 = cda40dc on main carried all F1–F6 — tree searches "proved" hardening absent. Read deployed code via `git show <deployed-sha>:<path>`; never trust the working tree for deployed behavior.
- **Timezone trap**: this Mac renders local time as UTC+12, yasyf-home as PDT — cross-host `ps lstart`/log comparison in local time fabricates 19-hour "anomalies" (cost a redundant remote helper-restart cycle this deploy; host UTC clocks actually agreed within 3s). Compare in UTC only.
- jj workspaces are not colocated: no `.git`, so `git`/`gh` fail there — `ccx vcs ship`'s CI watch must run from the colocated checkout.
- Codex-wrapper agents must be told to run codex exec in the FOREGROUND — a backgrounded codex dies silently when the wrapper session ends.

LWW semantics are unchanged by design: a live old-account tab on another host is genuinely newest and wins. When switching accounts, log out/close the old tab on the other Mac.

Extensions wipe (same evening) was NOT cookiesync: kill-storm → Chromium crash recovery razed extension registration across all four Arc profiles; cookiesync has zero write paths to extension state. Recovery = Web Store reinstall by ID (chrome.storage.local is keyed by ID and reattaches).
